seoTitle: How to Fix Wix Forms Not Working (Troubleshooting)
metaDescription: Troubleshoot Wix contact forms that aren't submitting, from field validation issues to notification permission problems.
intro: A contact form that silently fails to submit or notify can cost you real leads without you noticing. This guide covers the most common causes of Wix form failures, including field validation conflicts, disconnected email notifications, and hidden submission backlogs, and how to fix each one.

steps.heading: Open the affected form
steps.body: In your Wix editor, select the form component that's experiencing submission issues.

steps.heading: Open Form Settings
steps.body: Click the floating settings icon to access the preference diagnostics grid.

steps.heading: Audit email notification rules
steps.body: Check the Settings tab to confirm connected admin and team email addresses are still valid and authorized.

steps.heading: Check field validation rules
steps.body: Review individual field validation properties for conflicting constraints that could be blocking submissions.

steps.heading: Check the Submissions dashboard
steps.body: Open the submissions table to see if entries are actually arriving but just not triggering notifications.

steps.heading: Run a live diagnostic test
steps.body: Use the live preview simulator to submit a mock entry and confirm the full delivery pipeline works end to end.

faq.question: Why is my form submitting but I'm not getting notified?
faq.answer: This usually points to an email notification setting that's misconfigured or an admin address that's no longer authorized — check the Email Notifications panel.

faq.question: Could a field validation rule be blocking submissions?
faq.answer: Yes, an accidental mandatory constraint or formatting rule on a field can prevent visitors from successfully submitting the form.

faq.question: How do I check if submissions are actually being received?
faq.answer: Open the Submissions table dashboard panel to see if entries are arriving even if notifications aren't firing.

faq.question: Should I republish after fixing a form issue?
faq.answer: Yes, run a final delivery verification test and then republish so the fix takes effect on your live site.
